steps.title: Add 200 μL of 1:1 20...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μL of 1:1 20 µM naringin:20 µM naringenin to each sample as your internal standard.

steps.title: In addition, add 200 μL of...
steps.instruction: In addition, add 200 μL of 1:1 naringin:naringenin to your 3 extra internal standard-only glass collection tubes. Dry these tubes immediately and store at -80 until analysis.

steps.title: Add 4 mL of 98:2 methanol:formic...
steps.instruction: Add 4 mL of 98:2 methanol:formic Acid to each sample tube. You can save time by using a Seripettor for this step. Image showing solvent being added to sample tubes.

steps.title: Cap each sample, then vortex for...
steps.instruction: Cap each sample, then vortex for ~30 seconds until samples are mixed

steps.title: If the sample pellet remains stuck...
steps.instruction: If the sample pellet remains stuck at the bottom of the tube, alternate between vortexing and vigorous handshaking until no pellet remains stuck at the bottom of the tub. Keep your index finger on the tube cap to minimize the chance of dropping a tube or liquid leaking out of the cap. Sample pellet stuck in the bottom of the tube will not be properly extracted as solvents cannot efficiently penetrate this tissue.

steps.title: Place samples in sonicator water bath
steps.instruction: Place samples in sonicator water bath. Sonicate the samples for 20 minutes for the first extraction round. For subsequent extraction rounds, sonicate for 10 minutes. Image showing a typical setup used to sonicate samples

steps.title: Centrifuge at 3700 rpm (~3200 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 3700 rpm (~3200 x g ) for 5 minutes at 4 ℃

steps.title: Decant supernatant of each sample into...
steps.instruction: Decant supernatant of each sample into their respective glass collection tube.

steps.title: Repeat Steps 6 through 10 two...
steps.instruction: Repeat Steps 6 through 10 two more times for a total of 3 extractions.

steps.title: By the end of the third...
steps.instruction: By the end of the third round, each glass collection tube should have ~12 mL of sample supernatant.

steps.title: Open the nitrogen evaporator luer valves
steps.instruction: Open the nitrogen evaporator luer valves. Make sure to close luer valves that will not be used. Note: this video shows the luer being moved from the closed position to the ideal open position (1.5 turns counterclockwise)

steps.title: Load glass collection sample tubes (including...
steps.instruction: Load glass collection sample tubes (including the 3 internal standard only tubes) into nitrogen evaporator. Lower each luer to be within the glass sample tube but still above the sample supernatant.

steps.title: Slowly open nitrogen gas regulator to...
steps.instruction: Slowly open nitrogen gas regulator to provide flow of nitrogen gas to each sample. Increase the flow until it is high enough to form dimples on the supernatant surface, but not high enough to cause splashing.

steps.title: Lower nitrogen evaporator sample rack into...
steps.instruction: Lower nitrogen evaporator sample rack into the warm bath water (pre-heated to 35 ℃) if faster evaporation is desired. Ensure that locking knob is fully tightened once the sample rack is lowered.

steps.title: Wait for samples to dry down,...
steps.instruction: Wait for samples to dry down, but check on the samples' progress once every ~30 min. The samples should take about 3 hours to dry in the evaporator.

steps.title: As liquid level goes down, you...
steps.instruction: As liquid level goes down, you can increase the flow rate to speed up the drying process, but make sure not to cause splashing/sputtering.

steps.title: Once the liquid level of the...
steps.instruction: Once the liquid level of the sample collection tubes is very low (a few millimeters; mostly residual water and formic acid), turn off the nitrogen flow and unload the samples and internal standards.

steps.title: Load samples and internal standards into RapidVap
steps.instruction: Load samples and internal standards into RapidVap. Dry samples with a speed setting of 35 and a starting pressure of 200 mbar.

steps.title: Stair-step the RapidVap pressure down in...
steps.instruction: Stair-step the RapidVap pressure down in 20 mbar steps every ~10 minutes.

steps.title: After stair-stepping down, remove the remaining...
steps.instruction: After stair-stepping down, remove the remaining moisture by setting the pressure to 10 mbar for 15 minutes.

steps.title: Unload and then cap samples and internal standard tubes
steps.instruction: Unload and then cap samples and internal standard tubes. Store dried-down samples and internal standards at -80 ℃ until ready for analysis. Dried and closed flavonoid samples that are ready to be placed in the -80.

steps.title: To each sample tube, add 5...
steps.instruction: To each sample tube, add 5 mL of LC-MS grade 1:1 methanol:water + 0.1% formic acid

steps.title: Sonicate each sample for ~10 seconds
steps.instruction: Sonicate each sample for ~10 seconds. Rapidly swirl each tube by hand while sonicating to help get dried sample off walls of the tube.

steps.title: Vortex each sample for ~5 seconds...
steps.instruction: Vortex each sample for ~5 seconds ensuring liquid reaches the upper parts of the tube without getting to the top.

steps.title: For each sample, label an 8...
steps.instruction: For each sample, label an 8 mL glass vial and add 200 μL of 20 μM taxifolin to each 8 mL vial Image of the sample tube, 8 mL dilution tube, LC vial, syringe, and filter needed for the following steps.

steps.title: Add an additional 3800 μL of...
steps.instruction: Add an additional 3800 μL of LC-MS grade 1:1 methanol:water + 0.1% formic acid

steps.title: Briefly mix original redissolved sample and...
steps.instruction: Briefly mix original redissolved sample and pipette 1 ml of redissolved sample into the mixture described above in the 8 mL vial.

steps.title: With a 3 ml syringe, aspirate ~2
steps.instruction: With a 3 ml syringe, aspirate ~2.5 ml of the diluted sample in addition to a little bit of air. Image of a 3 mL syringe with aspirated sample and additional ~0.5 mL of air.

steps.title: Safely recap the needle, unscrew it...
steps.instruction: Safely recap the needle, unscrew it from the syringe, and attach a 13 mm 0.20 μm PTFE filter

steps.title: Filter sample into a labeled 2 ml glass LC vial
steps.instruction: Filter sample into a labeled 2 ml glass LC vial. Cap each vial, then run samples on LC-MS/MS

steps.title: Pre-cool centrifuge to 4 ℃
steps.instruction: Pre-cool centrifuge to 4 ℃. Also pre-heat both the nitrogen sample concentrator water bath and the RapidVap to 35 ℃. Ensure there is sufficient nitrogen gas available for sample drying.

steps.title: Label 1 glass borosilicate culture tube...
steps.instruction: Label 1 glass borosilicate culture tube (16x125 mm) for each sample tube you have. In addition, label 3 extra tubes that will only contain your internal standards.

steps.title: Ensure valves for each sample are turned 1
steps.instruction: Ensure valves for each sample are turned 1.5 times counterclockwise from the closed (12 o'clock) position. The red dot will now be at the 6 o'clock position. This practice ensures even nitrogen flow from each luer.

steps.title: Embed all sample tubes and all...
steps.instruction: Embed all sample tubes and all glass collection tubes in a tub of ice to keep the samples cool. Typical setup for keeping samples on ice.

steps.title: Wipe each needle with a KimWipe...
steps.instruction: Wipe each needle with a KimWipe soaked in IPA to clean any potential residue from a previous user.

steps.title: The luer does not need to...
steps.instruction: The luer does not need to be particularly close to the surface of the liquid for effective evaporation. Often, the end of the luer will be 1 - 2" away from the liquid surface.

steps.title: Ensure that the nitrogen regulator is...
steps.instruction: Ensure that the nitrogen regulator is in the lowest flow position before opening the tank to prevent sudden influx of gas that can cause samples to splash out of tubes/cross-contaminate. Should this happen, the extracts are no longer quantitative and cannot be used for analysis.

steps.title: Wipe the luers again with a...
steps.instruction: Wipe the luers again with a KimWipe soaked in IPA to clean off any residue that may have been left behind by previous samples.

steps.title: Ensure that each sample is dry...
steps.instruction: Ensure that each sample is dry by manually inspecting the bottom of each vial. Residual liquid can throw-off final volume estimates leading to quantification errors. If any moisture remains, place it back into the RapidVap for an additional 10 minutes at 10 mbar.

steps.title: Allow samples to warm slightly (Temperature...
steps.instruction: Allow samples to warm slightly (Temperature should be around ~4 ℃). Attempting to open tubes while they are at -80 ℃ can result in cracking or shattering of tubes and causes condensation to rapidly form inside.

steps.title: Pre-weigh 20 mg of freeze dried...
steps.instruction: Pre-weigh 20 mg of freeze dried plant powder (± 1 mg) into a 15 mL snap-cap tube.

steps.title: Total volume in the 8 mL...
steps.instruction: Total volume in the 8 mL vial is now 5 mL with a final composition of 4% taxifolin and the concentration of plant flavonoids diluted 5-fold. This dilution brings the concentration of plant flavonoids into the linear range of the LC-MS/MS platform, dilutes potential matrix compounds that interfere with analysis, and allows the user to work with a reasonable amount of starting sample to lower the contribution of weighing errors. For the purposes of quantification, the true redissolve volume is equivalent to 25 mL given the starting volume of 5 mL and 5-fold dilution. Graphical depiction of 5x dilution strategy described above.

steps.title: Use the air bubble inside the...
steps.instruction: Use the air bubble inside the syringe to push out any liquid trapped in the filter, if needed.

steps.title: If the RapidVap is very easily...
steps.instruction: If the RapidVap is very easily going below the set target pressure, you can go ahead and proceed to a lower pressure sooner than 10 minutes.

steps.title: Alternatively, if the RapidVap continues to...
steps.instruction: Alternatively, if the RapidVap continues to face resistance to reaching the target pressure, leave it set to that pressure until it no longer faces significant resistance.

steps.title: We highly recommend using a two-stage...
steps.instruction: We highly recommend using a two-stage regulator to ensure consistent gas delivery pressure as the internal pressure of the tank decreases.

steps.title: If samples are not run immediately,...
steps.instruction: If samples are not run immediately, store in a -20 ℃ freezer to keep samples in a cold, dark place. Samples will not freeze at this temperature due to methanol, but the cold and darkness will minimize oxidation. Long term storage in a -80 ℃ freezer is possible as samples will freeze at this temperature.

steps.title: Move the location of samples around...
steps.instruction: Move the location of samples around each extraction to minimize effects of "hot spots" on extraction efficiency.

steps.title: Add ice to the water bath...
steps.instruction: Add ice to the water bath to keep the samples cool

steps.title: The universal sample holder seen above...
steps.instruction: The universal sample holder seen above can be downloaded and 3D printed from the following link: https://3d.nih.gov/entries/3DPX-021167

steps.title: The modified rack seen above can...
steps.instruction: The modified rack seen above can be downloaded and 3D printed from the following link: https://3d.nih.gov/entries/3DPX-021167

steps.title: The sample organizer seen above can...
steps.instruction: The sample organizer seen above can be download and 3D printed from the following link: http://3d.nih.gov/entries/3DPX-020366
